brief_justification: Arrives at exactly $19,160 matching the correct answer with valid application of elasticity formulas; goes one level beyond bare calculation by noting the net revenue shift and individual product contributions, but does not explore second-order effects or model assumptions; proceeds linearly without naming any biases, checking assumptions about elasticity linearity, or reflecting on the reasoning method; performs basic implicit verification by reconciling component revenue changes ($-2,720 and $+2,880) against the net $+160 result; presents conclusions with full confidence and never acknowledges that point-elasticity approximation may not hold over a 40% price change or that constant elasticity is assumed; considers only the direct formula-application path with no mention of arc elasticity, midpoint method, or alternative interpretations; every step is shown explicitly with all inputs traced from the problem data, fully reproducible by a third party.
